Checks: 7 0

Knit directory: MT_Project/

This reproducible R Markdown analysis was created with workflowr (version 1.7.2). The Checks tab describes the reproducibility checks that were applied when the results were created. The Past versions tab lists the development history.

R Markdown file: up-to-date

Great! Since the R Markdown file has been committed to the Git repository, you know the exact version of the code that produced these results.

Environment: empty

Great job! The global environment was empty. Objects defined in the global environment can affect the analysis in your R Markdown file in unknown ways. For reproduciblity it’s best to always run the code in an empty environment.

Seed: set.seed(20260612)

The command set.seed(20260612) was run prior to running the code in the R Markdown file. Setting a seed ensures that any results that rely on randomness, e.g. subsampling or permutations, are reproducible.

Session information: recorded

Great job! Recording the operating system, R version, and package versions is critical for reproducibility.

Cache: none

Nice! There were no cached chunks for this analysis, so you can be confident that you successfully produced the results during this run.

File paths: relative

Great job! Using relative paths to the files within your workflowr project makes it easier to run your code on other machines.

Repository version: bee068d

Great! You are using Git for version control. Tracking code development and connecting the code version to the results is critical for reproducibility.

The results in this page were generated with repository version bee068d. See the Past versions tab to see a history of the changes made to the R Markdown and HTML files.

Note that you need to be careful to ensure that all relevant files for the analysis have been committed to Git prior to generating the results (you can use wflow_publish or wflow_git_commit). workflowr only checks the R Markdown file, but you know if there are other scripts or data files that it depends on. 2 Get the Data

custom_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("#005EA8", "#6DB335", "#FFD966", "#EDAAD2", "#9F1C67"))
gradient_colors3 <- custom_palette(6)
gradient_colors4 <- custom_palette(4)
gradient_colors3 <- custom_palette(3)

3 Functions

Volcano_Plots <- function(Data, Title, Subtitle, pCutoff = 0.05, FCcutoff = 0.5) {
  
  # Clean extreme values
  df <- Data
  df <- df[is.finite(df$avg_log2FC) & is.finite(df$p_val_adj), ]
  
  # dynamic axis limits
  xmax <- max(df$avg_log2FC, na.rm = TRUE)
  xmin <- min(df$avg_log2FC, na.rm = TRUE)
  ymax <- max(-log10(df$p_val_adj), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Add padding
  xpad <- (xmax - xmin) * 0.1
  ypad <- ymax * 0.1
  
  combined_subtitle <- paste0(Subtitle, " ● Thresholds: FDR < ", pCutoff, ", |Log2FC| > ", FCcutoff)
  
  EV <- EnhancedVolcano(
    df,
    lab = df$gene,
    x = 'avg_log2FC',
    y = 'p_val_adj',
    xlab = 'log2 Foldchange',
    ylab = "-log10 FDR",
    legendLabels = c('NS','log2FC','FDR','FDR & log2FC'),
    col = c("darkgrey", "#005EA8", "#FFD966", "#9F1C67"),
    title = Title,
    subtitle = combined_subtitle,   
    xlim = c(xmin - xpad, xmax + xpad),
    ylim = c(0, ymax + ypad),
    pCutoff = pCutoff,              
    FCcutoff = FCcutoff,            

    cutoffLineType = 'dashed',      
    cutoffLineCol = 'black',        
    cutoffLineWidth = 0.6,
    labSize = 3.0,
    drawConnectors = TRUE,
    widthConnectors = 0.5
  )
  
  return(EV)
}

##################################### Tables

create_table <- function(df) {
  DT::datatable(
    df,
    extensions = 'Buttons', 
    options = list(
      dom = 'Blfrtip',                       
      buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf'),
      pageLength = 10,                      
      lengthMenu = c(10, 25, 50, 100)     
    ),
    filter = 'top',                         
    class = 'cell-border stripe hover'       
  )
}

4 Gene Overlap

4.1 Get all the DEG

Cutoffs Chosen: FDR <= 0.01, abs(log2FC) > 1

6753          ELOVL6 9.999653e-01 -0.1521465 0.482 0.434 1.000000e+00
upset_up_genes   <- list()
upset_down_genes <- list()


p_cutoff  <- 0.01
fc_cutoff <- 1

for (comp_name in names(DEG.lists)) {

  df <- DEG.lists[[comp_name]]
  
  if (!"gene" %in% colnames(df)) {
    df <- df %>% rownames_to_column(var = "gene")
  }
  
  upset_up_genes[[comp_name]] <- df %>%
    filter(p_val_adj <= p_cutoff & avg_log2FC >= fc_cutoff) %>%
    pull(gene) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  upset_down_genes[[comp_name]] <- df %>%
    filter(p_val_adj <= p_cutoff & avg_log2FC <= -fc_cutoff) %>%
    pull(gene) %>%
    na.omit()
}

# one master object
upset_final_data <- list(
  Up_Regulated   = upset_up_genes,
  Down_Regulated = upset_down_genes
)

4.1.1 UpsetPlot for all Groups

################################################### Upregulated Intersections

upset(
  fromList(upset_final_data$Up_Regulated), 
  nsets = 4,                       
  order.by = "freq",               
  main.bar.color = "#9F1C67",      
  sets.bar.color = "darkgrey",     
  text.scale = c(1.3, 1.3, 1, 1, 1.2, 1) 
)

################################################### Downregulated Intersections

upset(
  fromList(upset_final_data$Down_Regulated), 
  nsets = 4, 
  order.by = "freq", 
  main.bar.color = "#005EA8", 
  sets.bar.color = "darkgrey",
  text.scale = c(1.3, 1.3, 1, 1, 1.2, 1)
)

4.1.2 Venn diagram of all ovelapping genes

################################# upregulated genes

ggvenn(
  data = upset_final_data$Up_Regulated, 
  columns = names(upset_final_data$Up_Regulated),                          
  fill_color = gradient_colors4, 
  stroke_size = 0.5, 
  set_name_size = 4
) +
  labs(
    title = "Ovelap of sign. Upregulated Genes",
    subtitle = paste0("Fibroblast Differential Expression ● Cutoffs: FDR < ", p_cutoff, ", |Log2FC| > ", fc_cutoff)
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "bold", size = 16, color = "black"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "italic", size = 11, color = "dimgray")
  )

################################# downregulated genes

ggvenn(
  data = upset_final_data$Down_Regulated, 
  columns = names(upset_final_data$Down_Regulated),                          
  fill_color = gradient_colors4, 
  stroke_size = 0.5, 
  set_name_size = 4
) +
  labs(
    title = "Ovelap of sign. Downregulated Genes",
    subtitle = paste0("Fibroblast Differential Expression ● Cutoffs: FDR < ", p_cutoff, ", |Log2FC| > ", fc_cutoff)
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "bold", size = 16, color = "black"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "italic", size = 11, color = "dimgray")
  )

4.2 Heatmap try

library(circlize)
========================================
circlize version 0.4.18
CRAN page: https://cran.r-project.org/package=circlize
Github page: https://github.com/jokergoo/circlize
Documentation: https://jokergoo.github.io/circlize_book/book/

If you use it in published research, please cite:
Gu, Z. circlize implements and enhances circular visualization
  in R. Bioinformatics 2014.

This message can be suppressed by:
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(circlize))
========================================
FDR <- 0.01 

# Extract gene lists and filter only for significance
processed_lists <- lapply(names(DEG.lists), function(name) {
  df <- DEG.lists[[name]]
  df %>%
    filter(p_val_adj < FDR) %>%
    dplyr::select(gene, avg_log2FC) %>%
    rename(!!name := avg_log2FC) 
})

# Merge all 4 data frames together by gene name
# Genes not significant in a comparison will temporarily become NA
merged_df <- purrr::reduce(processed_lists, full_join, by = "gene")

# Convert NAs to 0 (meaning 0 log2FC change when not significant)
merged_df[is.na(merged_df)] <- 0

# Convert to a matrix and set gene names as row names
fc_matrix <- as.matrix(merged_df[,-1])
rownames(fc_matrix) <- merged_df$gene

# Optional: If your matrix is huge (e.g., > 2000 genes), you can subset 
# to the top most variable or top significant genes for visual clarity.
# Example: Keep top 200 genes based on variance across the 4 conditions
if (nrow(fc_matrix) > 200) {
  top_genes <- head(order(apply(fc_matrix, 1, var), decreasing = TRUE), 200)
  fc_matrix_subset <- fc_matrix[top_genes, ]
} else {
  fc_matrix_subset <- fc_matrix
}

max_val <- max(abs(fc_matrix_subset))
col_fun = colorRamp2(c(-max_val, 0, max_val), c("#005EA8", "white", "#9F1C67"))

# --- Step 3: Generate the Heatmap ---


Heatmap(
  fc_matrix_subset, 
  name = "log2FC", 
  col = col_fun,
  
  # Clustering options
  cluster_columns = FALSE,        
  cluster_rows = TRUE,            
  show_row_dend = TRUE,           
  
  # Text adjustments & Axes Rotation
  column_names_rot = 45,          
  column_names_side = "bottom",   
  show_row_names = FALSE,          
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8), 
  
  # Segmentation
  row_km = 4,                     
  column_title = "Macrophage Modulation of TGF-Beta Response"
)

4.3 Check on genes that are upredulated in Baseline TGF_Beta but not or downregulated in Modulated TGF-Beta

FDR <- 0.01 

merged_df <- purrr::reduce(processed_lists, full_join, by = "gene")
